description: This weak-beam dark-field (WBDF) transmission electron micrograph displays a dissociated dislocation in the base Fe-Ni-Cr austenitic steel (Fe-15Ni-11Cr base composition), serving as the representative imaging condition for measuring partial dislocation separation to determine stacking fault energy (SFE). The micrograph shows two partial dislocations separated by distance d (indicated by double-headed arrow), with annotations defining α as the angle between the dislocation line vector and the Burgers vector b of the undissociated dislocation, and the diffraction vector g parallel to b (g//b two-beam condition). The top-right inset presents the corresponding selected area electron diffraction (SAED) pattern. With a 50 nm scale bar, this image exemplifies the methodology where multiple (d, α) data pairs were recorded along several dislocations and input into isotropic elasticity theory equations to calculate the base alloy SFE of 26.3 ± 3.0 mJ m⁻². The figure establishes the experimental basis for comparing SFE variations between the base alloy and its 2.5 wt.% Si and 2.5 wt.% Al modified counterparts, demonstrating that Si decreases SFE while Al increases it.
